meaning: And The Last (as a name/attribute of Allah)
And The Apparent One (one of Allah's names)
and The Hidden One (one of Allah's names)
he goes out/exits (happening now or in future)
you all work/act/do (referring to the people being addressed in this ayah)
he causes to enter (the subject of this verb is Allah)
those who were made successors (of what others possessed)
in it (referring to what Allah made you stewards over)
for/to you all (addressing the disbelievers who don't believe)
he calls/invites you all (to believe in your Lord)
The one who/which - connects descriptions to what's being described
so that he brings you all out
with/to you all (referring to those being brought out of darkness)
from you all (referring to those being addressed about spending)
and he engaged in mutual combat (with others)
greater/more magnificent (than those who came after)
those who (referring to those who spent and fought mentioned in the ayah)
that (referring to the great success mentioned after)
let us get (some of your light)
between them (separating the believers from the hypocrites)
in it (referring to the wall/barrier with a gate mentioned)
and she (false hopes) deceived you all completely
so today (when no ransom will be accepted)
from them (referring to those who were given the Book)
those who break away from obedience (plural)
perhaps you all, so that you all
you all use reason (addressing the believers in this ayah)
the men who give charity (acted on)
and the women who give charity (acted on)
those (far away, pointing to the disbelievers mentioned)
and the earth (being compared to Paradise's width)
what (referring to what has escaped/passed you)
arrogant, self-admiring (describing someone Allah does not love)
and they order/command (the people with stinginess)
Allah (as the object being referred to)
The Completely Self-Sufficient (a name of Allah describing Him in this ayah)
so that he stands (purpose for sending messengers)
a mighty force (describing the iron mentioned in the ayah)
and in order that he knows/learns
offspring/descendants of them both (referring to Noah and Abraham)
then from them (referring to the offspring mentioned before)
those who depart from obedience (nominative plural)
then (connects this part to what came before)
from them (referring to those who believed)
they believed strongly (addressing the believers in this ayah)
you (all) walk (in the light that Allah gives you)
Most Forgiving (as an attribute of Allah)
and that (connects this emphasized statement to what came before)
